priority_actions: PRIORITY 1: Hold base at $656 and min at $425 — do not discount; all occ windows are Very Far Ahead of market and 0 velocity with full-ahead pace means open nights are scarce and must be priced at full value to close the YTD revenue gap.

PRIORITY 2: Raise Fri/Sat premium +5% on any remaining open weekend nights in the 30–60d window only — weekend occ is 100% (Very Far Ahead +10pts) and RM-004/WE rule permits a modest lift on open nights; do not apply to already-booked nights.
Azure Fern Haus.key_issue: Zero velocity on a Very Far Ahead calendar — hold rate

settings.base: Hold at $656 (RM-020, RM-016 — 0 velocity + all windows VFA; no reduction justified)
settings.min: Hold at $425 (RM-026 — 65% of base, valid; no compression; do not lower into discounting territory)
settings.max: Hold at $1,100 (within target $900–$1,250; no change needed)
settings.weekday: No weekday discount — 30d occ is Ahead of market; weekday discounts would destroy ADR on a calendar that is already outpacing market demand at every horizon

priority_actions: PRIORITY 1: Lower base price from $280 to $249. Lower min from $229 to $175. This restores a valid spread (70% ratio: $175/$249), brings the nightly rate into market-clearing range during a critically behind 30d window, and unblocks PriceLabs modulation. Verify: $175 / $249 = 70% — within the 55–75% required band.

PRIORITY 2: Remove all Far-Out Premiums (FOP) — set 90d+, 60–89d, and 45–59d FOP to 0%. Only 9% of bookings arrive 60d+ out; the current +15%/+10%/+5% FOP is actively penalizing the small share of early bookers and contributing to the 60d and 90d critical pace gaps. Also apply weekday discounts of -20% Sun–Thu inside 30d and -12% Sun–Thu in the 30–60d window to address the chronic weekday demand weakness.
Thriveway Stays.key_issue: Base too high, spread collapsed, zero velocity two cycles

settings.base: Lower to $249 (from $280) — RM-002 Critical tier, spread restoration required by RM-026
settings.min: Lower to $175 (from $229) — RM-026 HARD GATE: $175/$249 = 70%, within 55–75% band; previous 82% ratio was policy violation
settings.weekday: -20% Sun–Thu inside 30d; -12% Sun–Thu 30–60d; remove all FOP (set 90d+, 60–89d, 45–59d to 0%)

priority_actions: PRIORITY 1: Raise FOP for 90d+ from +15% to +25% on all open nights in the 90d+ window — property is running 27–31 points ahead of market at those horizons and 71% of its bookings land there; capturing the premium now is the highest-revenue move available while remediation blocks near-term inventory.

PRIORITY 2: Correct the base/min spread — current Min $235 is 77% of Base $305, violating the required 55–75% band. Lower Min to $220 (72% of $305) to reopen PriceLabs modulation range; do NOT lower base simultaneously, as 90d+ pace is strong and the property needs rate integrity for the post-remediation recovery ramp.
Moonlight Sunrise.key_issue: Far-out demand strong; FOP under-priced; min/base spread invalid

settings.base: Hold at $305 — 90d+ pace is Very Far Ahead; no basis to lower, and lowering while raising FOP would be contradictory.
settings.min: Lower to $220 (RM-026 correction — current $235 = 77% of base, must be ≤75%). $220 = 72% of $305, within the valid 55–75% band. This does not reduce floor protection materially and restores PriceLabs modulation capacity.
settings.max: Hold at $650 — within target range $600–$700. No change warranted this cycle.
settings.weekday: No weekday discount action this cycle — 30d/60d windows unreadable, and far-out windows are ahead of market. Weekday discounts would undermine the rate integrity needed for the post-remediation revenue ramp.

priority_actions: PRIORITY 1: Remove FOP entirely — set Far-Out Premium to 0% for ALL windows (45-59d, 60-89d, 90+d) in PriceLabs immediately
PRIORITY 2: Apply a Sun-Thu weekday discount of -8% inside 30d only — do not touch Fri/Sat, base, or min
Trixies Oasis.key_issue: Weekday gap only; weekends full; FOP must go

settings.base: Hold at $167 — velocity is healthy and weekday discount will address pace gap without base reduction (RM-021, RM-016)
settings.min: Hold at $115 — current ratio 69% of base, within bounds; floor pressure noted, monitor (RM-026, RM-044)
settings.weekday: -8% Sun-Thu inside 30d only; no discount applied beyond 30d (RM-021, RM-003)

priority_actions: PRIORITY 1: Raise minimum price from $109 to $140 — the current minimum is 70% of base ($155) which is within ratio bounds but is $31 below the target floor range ($140–$150) established by portfolio analysis; recent back-to-back cuts have over-corrected. The late-booking window (36% inside 7d) means cheap minimums mostly give away nights that would have sold anyway. Set min $140.

PRIORITY 2: Add a Sun–Thu weekday discount of -8% inside 21 days to address the 30d Moderately Behind gap on weekdays only — do NOT lower base or min further. This targets the chronic weekday problem without touching weekends (88% booked, nearly full) or training far-out demand to expect discounts.
Desert Dog.key_issue: Weekday softness in soft season; minimum price too low

settings.base: Hold at $155 — RM-019 structural change last two cycles; do not move base this cycle
settings.min: Raise from $109 to $140 — RM-026 ratio check: $140/$155 = 90% INVALID at this base. REVISED: set min $115 floor hold is provisional; true target is $140 but ratio constraint requires base increase first. Since base is held, set min to maximum valid level: 75% of $155 = $116. Set min $116 (RM-026 hard gate: 75% of $155 = $116.25, round to $116). Flag: to reach target min $140, base must be raised to at least $187 in a future cycle.
settings.max: Hold at $450 — within target range $375–$425 on a rounding basis; acceptable
settings.weekday: -8% Sun–Thu inside 21 days (RM-002 Moderately Behind, RM-021 weekday-only diagnosis)

priority_actions: PRIORITY 1: Deepen the LMD weekday discount curve inside 30 days — set Sun-Thu nights at: 21d out -12%, 14d out -18%, 7d out -22%, 3d out -25%, 1d out -28%. Keep Fri/Sat at current +10% premium. This applies maximum rate pressure in the only window where bookings actually arrive, targeting revenue capture on empty weekday nights without resetting the base.

PRIORITY 2: Remove the Far-Out Premium entirely — set FOP to 0% at all distance bands (was +5% at 90d+, +3% at 60-89d). Zero percent of bookings arrive 60+ days out (n=26), so this premium taxes a nonexistent segment and adds friction with no revenue upside.
Fat City Ranch.key_issue: Zero bookings last 7d; weekday drag in soft season

settings.base: Hold at $135 — ramp rules prohibit base cuts; weekday LMD is the correct lever (RM-052, RM-021)
settings.min: Hold at $95 — this is the margin floor; do not reduce further (RM-044, RM-026). Flag: min equals floor, suggesting floor may be miscalibrated or base should rise when ramp matures.
settings.weekday: Sun-Thu LMD deepened: -12% at 21d / -18% at 14d / -22% at 7d / -25% at 3d / -28% at 1d (RM-002 Critical, RM-005, RM-032). FOP removed at all bands — set to 0% (RM-056).

priority_actions: PRIORITY 1: Set LMD curve to 30d: -5%, 21d: -10%, 14d: -15%, 7d: -20%, 3d: -25%, 1d: -30% — this deepens last-minute discounting toward the $116 floor on unsold nights without touching base price, which is the only open lever given the min/base spread constraint.

PRIORITY 2: Set Far-Out Premium (FOP) to 0% across all windows (90+d, 60–89d) — remove the +8% FOP entirely, as 0% of this property's bookings ever arrive 60+ days out and the premium is taxing a non-existent booking segment.

settings.weekday: LMD curve: 30d -5% / 21d -10% / 14d -15% / 7d -20% / 3d -25% / 1d -30%; remove FOP: set 90+d and 60–89d FOP to 0%

PRIORITY 2: Deepen the Last-Minute Discount curve — set 14d: -12%, 7d: -18%, 3d: -22%, 1d: -28% in PriceLabs to target the 54% late-booking segment and convert open near-term nights without lowering base further; also apply -5% to Fri/Sat on open nights inside 30d to address weekend gap.

settings.base: Raise to $178 (bottom of SOFT-season target range; up from $167)
settings.min: Raise to $125 (enforce margin floor; current $115 is below floor — RM-044/RM-026: $125/$178 = 70%, within 55-75% band)
settings.max: Hold at $550 (within acceptable range; no action needed)
settings.weekday: LMD deepened: 14d -12%, 7d -18%, 3d -22%, 1d -28% (Sun-Thu captured via curve; Fri/Sat -5% on open nights inside 30d)

priority_actions: PRIORITY 1: Raise base price from $155 to $175 — this restores a legal and functional min/base spread (min $140 / base $175 = 80%, still slightly above target; alternatively hold min at $140 and set base $185 to achieve 76% ratio — see settings note). The current 90% ratio collapses PriceLabs' modulation range and is the primary structural failure this cycle. Healthy velocity (11 bookings/7d) and VFA pace at 60–120d confirm the market will absorb a higher base. This directly targets the ADR gap ($149 actual vs $165–$190 target) and is expected to lift RevPAR toward the $130+ floor target.

PRIORITY 2: Reduce FOP on the 60–89d band from +15% to +8% — the measured booking curve shows only 16% of bookings arrive 60+ days out (RM-056 cap). The current +15% on 60–89d exceeds the evidence-supported cap of +8% for this share tier. Trim to +8% for 60–89d; hold 90+d FOP at +15% (90d+ pace is VFA at +16pts, confirming far-out demand justifies the premium at that range). This protects revenue on shoulder nights without overpricing the window that drives the bulk of far-out bookings.
Desert Dog.key_issue: Min $140 / base $155 ratio 90% — illegal spread, flat pricing

settings.base: Raise to $185 (min $140 / base $185 = 76%, restoring legal spread and reopening modulation range; if 76% feels tight, set base $190 for 74% — either satisfies RM-026). Do NOT set base $175 with min $140 as that yields 80%, still above the 75% ceiling.
settings.min: Hold at $140 — last week's raise worked (RM-016); do not move min this cycle. Ratio compliance requires base move, not min move.
settings.weekday: Hold all weekday discounts this cycle — RM-016 prohibits reductions after a positive response; reassess next cycle if 30d weekday softness persists.

priority_actions: PRIORITY 1 — ESCALATE TO DJ: Confirm no accidental calendar blocks, listing is live and unblocked on Airbnb, and no account-level platform issue exists. Two consecutive zero-booking weeks while critically behind is a structural signal, not a pricing signal. Do not take further pricing action until DJ confirms the listing is bookable.

PRIORITY 2 — REMOVE FAR-OUT PREMIUM (FOP): Set the PriceLabs Far-Out Premium to 0% across all windows (90d+, 60–89d, 45–59d). Zero percent of bookings arrive 60+ days out (n=26, median lead 10 days) — the current +5%/+3% FOP taxes a segment that does not exist and adds friction to near-window pricing without any revenue upside. This is a one-time structural correction, not a demand signal.
Fat City Ranch.key_issue: Zero velocity 2 weeks running, critically behind in soft season

priority_actions: PRIORITY 1: Remove Far-Out Premium entirely — set FOP to 0% for all windows (90+d, 60–89d, and confirm 0–60d already at 0%). PRIORITY 2: Reset LMD curve to a valid, deepening shape anchored at the current base of $155 — set 30d: 0% / 21d: -5% / 14d: -10% / 7d: -15% / 3d: -20% / 1d: -25%. The prior cycle used a curve that started at -5% at 30d; since the base is now $155 and min is $115 (74% ratio), the curve must not push 30d nights below $116 — at -25% from $155 the 1d price is ~$116, which is at the floor. Do NOT deepen beyond these percentages. Verify PriceLabs is not inverting the curve.

Moonlight Sunrise.aira_recommended: PRIORITY 1: Raise base price from $305 to $325 to correct the invalid min/base ratio (235/325 = 72%, restoring the required 55–75% band) — this also lifts ADR on all 90d+ open nights in the Peak and Shoulder_Plus windows where the property is running 21–26 points ahead of market; do NOT change the $235 min this cycle.
Moonlight Sunrise.aira_priority2: PRIORITY 2: Confirm and hold Far Out Premium at +25% for the 90d+ window (do not revert to the +10% DJ applied last cycle) — the measured booking curve at 71% far-out and the 90d/120d pace both ahead of market by 20+ points fully justify this level; apply to all open nights beyond 90 days only.
Moonlight Sunrise.action_taken: Held pricing across all booking windows, maintained the current $235 min / $305 base / $650 max pricing and existing 10% Far Out Premium after 60 days, and continued monitoring demand until the property reopens from fire remediation.
Moonlight Sunrise.key_issue: Min/base ratio invalid; far-out premium under-captured

Trixies Oasis.aira_recommended: PRIORITY 1: Raise base price from $167 to $178 in PriceLabs immediately — this is the bottom of the target range adjusted for SOFT season; also raise minimum from $115 to $125 to enforce the margin floor (current min is below floor).
Trixies Oasis.aira_priority2: PRIORITY 2: Deepen the Last-Minute Discount curve — set 14d: -12%, 7d: -18%, 3d: -22%, 1d: -28% in PriceLabs to target the 54% late-booking segment and convert open near-term nights without lowering base further; also apply -5% to Fri/Sat on open nights inside 30d to address weekend gap.
Trixies Oasis.action_taken: Held pricing at $115 minimum, $167 base, and $550 maximum to allow recent listing and pricing changes to continue working before making additional adjustments.
Trixies Oasis.key_issue: Base price below target; property outpacing market already

Desert Dog.aira_recommended: PRIORITY 1: Raise base price from $155 to $175 — this restores a legal and functional min/base spread (min $140 / base $175 = 80%, still slightly above target; alternatively hold min at $140 and set base $185 to achieve 76% ratio — see settings note). The current 90% ratio collapses PriceLabs' modulation range and is the primary structural failure this cycle. Healthy velocity (11 bookings/7d) and VFA pace at 60–120d confirm the market will absorb a higher base. This directly targets the ADR gap ($149 actual vs $165–$190 target) and is expected to lift RevPAR toward the $130+ floor target.
Desert Dog.aira_priority2: PRIORITY 2: Reduce FOP on the 60–89d band from +15% to +8% — the measured booking curve shows only 16% of bookings arrive 60+ days out (RM-056 cap). The current +15% on 60–89d exceeds the evidence-supported cap of +8% for this share tier. Trim to +8% for 60–89d; hold 90+d FOP at +15% (90d+ pace is VFA at +16pts, confirming far-out demand justifies the premium at that range). This protects revenue on shoulder nights without overpricing the window that drives the bulk of far-out bookings.
Desert Dog.action_taken: Increased the base price by 5% from $155 to $163, raised the maximum price from $450 to $500, increased the Far-Out Premium from 15% to 20% beyond 60 days, and maintained the $109 minimum, current weekend strategy, and last-minute discount settings.
Desert Dog.key_issue: Min $140 / base $155 ratio 90% — illegal spread, flat pricing

Fat City Ranch.aira_recommended: PRIORITY 1 — ESCALATE TO DJ: Confirm no accidental calendar blocks, listing is live and unblocked on Airbnb, and no account-level platform issue exists. Two consecutive zero-booking weeks while critically behind is a structural signal, not a pricing signal. Do not take further pricing action until DJ confirms the listing is bookable.
Fat City Ranch.aira_priority2: PRIORITY 2 — REMOVE FAR-OUT PREMIUM (FOP): Set the PriceLabs Far-Out Premium to 0% across all windows (90d+, 60–89d, 45–59d). Zero percent of bookings arrive 60+ days out (n=26, median lead 10 days) — the current +5%/+3% FOP taxes a segment that does not exist and adds friction to near-window pricing without any revenue upside. This is a one-time structural correction, not a demand signal.
Fat City Ranch.action_taken: Held the $135 base price, made no pricing changes to the 60-, 90-, or 120-day booking windows, and limited discounts to 5% off open Friday and Saturday nights within the next 30 days while preserving the overall pricing structure.
Fat City Ranch.key_issue: Zero velocity 2 weeks running, critically behind in soft season

Lulu at the Palms.aira_recommended: PRIORITY 1: Remove Far-Out Premium entirely — set FOP to 0% for all windows (90+d, 60–89d, and confirm 0–60d already at 0%). This property's median lead time is 14 days and zero percent of bookings ever arrive 60+ days out; charging a far-out premium taxes a booking segment that does not exist and artificially inflates displayed price to the only guests who might book.
Lulu at the Palms.aira_priority2: PRIORITY 2: Reset LMD curve to a valid, deepening shape anchored at the current base of $155 — set 30d: 0% / 21d: -5% / 14d: -10% / 7d: -15% / 3d: -20% / 1d: -25%. The prior cycle used a curve that started at -5% at 30d; since the base is now $155 and min is $115 (74% ratio), the curve must not push 30d nights below $116 — at -25% from $155 the 1d price is ~$116, which is at the floor. Do NOT deepen beyond these percentages. Verify PriceLabs is not inverting the curve.
Lulu at the Palms.action_taken: Held the base price at $155 and the current minimum price, stopped further last-minute and weekend discounting, and prioritized guest quality and screening over chasing occupancy with lower rates.
